features.title: pi-context clone — import a substrate from another project as a content-addressed fork
features.description: A dual-surface command (library cloneSubstrate + Pi tool + CLI + orchestrator script) that copies a source substrate's content (config vocabulary + all blocks + relations.json + objects/) into a destination dir, mints a fresh substrate_id, records a substrate_derived_from_substrate edge to the source, and writes a provenance-bearing registry entry. Fork semantics preserve the one-id<->one-dir invariant. Per DEC-0002.
acceptance_criteria: cloneSubstrate copies config + all blocks + relations.json + objects/ into the destination dir
the destination receives a freshly minted substrate_id distinct from the source
a substrate_derived_from_substrate edge records destination-derived-from-source lineage
the destination registry entry carries origin/provenance (derived-from source id + imported_at)
no substrate_id is duplicated across dirs (one-id<->one-dir SoT invariant holds)
surfaced as pi-context clone <source> <dest-dir> across library + Pi tool + CLI + orchestrator script
context-validate is clean on the cloned substrate

features.title: pi-context git merge driver (+ post-merge integrity pass) — structure-aware 3-way merge of substrate files under git
features.description: A custom git merge driver (registered via .gitattributes + a [merge "pi-context"] driver entry) that performs structure-aware per-file 3-way merge of substrate files (<block>.json, relations.json, config, schemas) from git's %O/%A/%B — items by stable id/oid, edges as a set, ordered ref-fields via the convergent sequence field-type, config/schema — writing a data-model-valid result; plus a post-merge hook / merge-finalize command for cross-block referential integrity + invariant/lens revalidation. Git keeps branches, merge-base, refs, cherry-pick. Per DEC-0004.
acceptance_criteria: Registered as a git merge driver via .gitattributes; on a same-repo branch merge git invokes it per substrate file with ancestor (%O) / ours (%A) / theirs (%B)
Items merge by stable id/oid not array index; a concurrent reorder or edit on both sides yields a data-model-valid result rather than line-mangled JSON
Edges merge by 3-way set semantics; objects/ union via git's directory merge (distinct hashes don't collide)
Ordered ref-fields merge via the convergent sequence field-type (FEAT-003) on the field's three versions
The post-merge pass enforces cross-block referential integrity and the full invariant + lens-validator pass; failures surface as a typed conflict set
Merge-base, branches, and cherry-pick remain git's; the driver only makes git's per-file result valid

features.title: Convergent ordered-sequence field-kind — CRDT-backed ordered item-refs that merge deterministically under concurrent insert/delete/move
features.description: A declared sequence-field kind over item-refs: a non-interleaving Fugue-class sequence CRDT (dense, immutable per-element position identifiers) plus a Kleppmann move-CRDT position register resolved by hybrid logical clock, with a sort-on-read projection to a plain ordered ref-array and array-position hand-edits translated to CRDT ops. CRDT state lives in the owning <block>.json; projection + field-aware merge live in block-api. One field-kind plugin into the substrate merge driver (FEAT-002). Per DEC-0005.
acceptance_criteria: Two divergent versions each insert, delete, and/or move elements in the same collection; the merge yields one replica-independent sequence containing all inserts, with no element duplicated or lost and no interleaving of concurrently-inserted runs
Insert mints one position identifier and mutates no neighbor
Move rewrites one element's position register; concurrent moves of the same element resolve deterministically by hybrid logical clock
The sort-on-read projection reproduces single-writer authoring order identically to a plain array, and accepts array-position hand-edits translated to CRDT operations

features.title: Substrate-derived state — config-driven context-current-state / context-status
features.description:
Today these ops hardwire their derivation to the stock kinds — focus / in-flight / ranked-next are computed from `framework-gaps` + `tasks`. On a custom-vocabulary substrate (e.g. `.workshopping` with `prompt-fragment` + `prompt-spec`) they return empty/inert: the substrate has a real "where are we / what's next," but the op can't see it because it's looking for kinds that aren't there.

Proposal: derive the derivation from active substrate's `config.json`, not from hardcoded kind names. The substrate declares its state-bearing vocabulary — which kind+predicate yields *focus*, which field gives *order/ranking*, which field+values mark *open/blocked/done*. Stock substrates declare `framework-gaps`+`tasks` (today's behavior preserved exactly); a custom substrate declares its own (e.g. `prompt-spec` ranked by `order`, blocked by unmet `deps`). With no declaration the op is a truthful "not configured," not a silent empty.

"where are we / what's next" becomes a capability *any* substrate opts into by declaring its own state fields, instead of a privilege of the stock project-management vocabulary.
features.motivation: context-current-state is fully inert on a custom-vocabulary substrate (confirmed: returns empty focus/inFlight/nextActions/blocked against .context-jit-spec-v2 whose state-bearing kinds are axioms/concepts/dispatch-modes, not tasks/framework-gaps), because currentState() hardcodes the stock kinds + the task_depends_on_task blocked-by relation + a P0-P3 rank. The where-are-we/what-next capability is thus a privilege of the stock PM vocabulary rather than something any substrate can opt into.
acceptance_criteria: Stock substrates (declaring framework-gaps + tasks) produce byte-identical context-current-state / context-status to today (behavior preserved exactly via the shipped conception declaring the stock state vocabulary).

A custom-vocabulary substrate that declares its own state-bearing vocabulary (which kind+predicate yields focus, which field gives order/ranking, which field+values mark open/blocked/done, which relation_type is blocked-by) gets focus / in-flight / ranked-next / blocked derived from ITS kinds.
With no state-derivation declaration, the ops return a truthful not-configured signal, not a silent empty result.
The derivation reads the active substrate config.json, with no hardcoded stock kind names / fields / status values / relation_types in the derivation path.

features.title: pi-context pi-bound: launch a pi session bound to the composed extension tool surface
features.description: A globally-runnable `pi-context pi-bound` command launches an interactive pi session restricted to the composed extension tool surface — the static SKILL.md tool union plus the config-declared per-target composites — reproducing scripts/launch-constrained-pi.sh as a first-class CLI command and subsuming it.
features.motivation: The constrained-launch capability exists only as an out-of-tree shell script that needs the repo checkout. As a command on a globally installed pi-context, it runs from any project with the published packages present.
acceptance_criteria: [orchestrator] From a cwd outside the repo with the packages installed, `pi-context pi-bound` launches an interactive pi session whose available tools are exactly the composed union (the two-package-root SKILL.md tool set plus config.tool_operations[] composites), equivalent to scripts/launch-constrained-pi.sh.
[orchestrator] In that session the agent can invoke the extension tools in the union and cannot invoke tools outside it.
[subagent] `--grant <id>` extends the union for that launch; passthrough arguments reach pi unchanged.

features.title: pi-context update — a unified, drift-aware, customization-preserving update command with --dry-run
features.description: A single `pi-context update` command brings an installed substrate's model (schemas + catalog-origin blocks) to a newer pi-context release while preserving the user's customizations: it consults the checkStatus drift classification, preserves or merges locally-modified schemas instead of overwriting (FGAP-046), surfaces every mutation it makes, and with --dry-run previews the full change set without writing. It supersedes the bare `/context install --update` flag as the user-facing update path, and states the guarantee that config registries, populated blocks, and user-added elements are never clobbered.
features.motivation: The framework exists to be customized; an update must not punish customization. Today update is a flag on install that overwrites locally-modified schemas, side-writes migration declarations unsurfaced, and never consults the drift detector that already exists. A unified command with a dry-run preview makes update safe and legible.
acceptance_criteria: [orchestrator] pi-context update --dry-run reports the full set of schema/block/migration changes it would make (per the checkStatus drift classification) and writes nothing.

[orchestrator] pi-context update preserves or merges locally-modified schemas (per FGAP-046's chosen route) rather than overwriting them; config registries, populated blocks, and user-added elements are untouched.
[orchestrator] Every mutation the command performs — schema re-syncs, migration-declaration registrations, block starters — is enumerated in its output.
[subagent] The command is exposed on the globally-installed pi-context (gated on the CLI grounding).

features.title: Convention-articulation enforcement — the validator requires planning artifacts to cite a governing convention or a missing-convention gap
features.description: context-validate requires every decision, feature, and task to articulate its governance: an item_governed_by_convention edge to a convention, OR an item_acknowledges_missing_convention edge to a 'no convention governs X' framework-gap. A planning artifact filed with neither is a validation violation, so conventions cannot be silently skipped and a missing convention surfaces as tracked debt rather than a silent opt-out.
features.motivation: Conventions govern planning before implementation, but their composition into context relies on the LLM reading and heeding them. Enforcing articulation at validation makes governance a checked precondition, not an aspiration.
acceptance_criteria: [orchestrator] A decision/feature/task with neither an item_governed_by_convention nor an item_acknowledges_missing_convention edge is flagged by context-validate.
[orchestrator] An item edged to a convention, or to a missing-convention gap, passes.
[subagent] The mechanism is config-only — two relation_types + three requires-edge invariants — with no validator code change.
[orchestrator] .context enforces at severity error (clean after backfill); the shipped catalog enforces at severity warning for fresh substrates.

features.title: pi-context-cli best-of-breed surface — script-parity input/output, discoverability, and write-safety to a superior CLI UX
features.description: The reflecting pi-context CLI is both the dogfooding surface and the new-user entry point; today it lags its op-twin orchestrator scripts on input ergonomics, human output, discoverability, error UX, and write-safety. This feature brings the CLI to best-of-breed parity additively. INVARIANT (every task preserves it): the reflecting CLI is additive — every currently-working invocation keeps working, and every in-pi op's behavior plus the --json data envelope are byte-unchanged; new affordances (kebab/alias flags, input shorthands, render modes, previews, granular exit codes, help/version) are layers over the canonical op surface, never replacements. The sole authorized in-pi op change is the read-schema addressed-node correction (FGAP-027), paired with its CLI-side twin so read-schema is coherent across both surfaces. A behavioral parity gate locks the input/output parity classes at commit so the regression class is caught-at-commit, not found-by-dogfooding.
features.motivation: The CLI is the surface the project is built and demonstrated on; every parity/discoverability/safety gap degrades both dogfooding and first-run UX. The standard is best-of-breed with a superior user experience, sequenced by priority, nothing tiered as ship-after.
acceptance_criteria: Input parity: the CLI accepts conventional flag forms (kebab-case, --id aliasing the op's single id-param, the scripts' --writer kind:id / --where field:op:value / CSV shorthands) and derives arrayKey from config — without changing any in-pi op.
Output + error parity: the CLI renders human output (--format) and field-named validation guidance with granular exit codes distinguishing error classes.

Discoverability: --help is scannable and grouped and surfaces every command including process modes (e.g. pi-bound); --version reports the package version; and each <op> --help renders a best-of-breed template (synopsis + typed flags + examples + related + footer) with machine-readable --help --format json.
Write-safety: --show-schema previews a block contract and append-block-item --dry-run validates the prospective whole file, each before any write, with no op change.
Read coherence: an addressed read returns the full subtree on BOTH the CLI and the in-pi read-schema op (the one authorized op change), 50KB cap retained.

Durability: a behavioral parity gate (parity-check.ts) FAILs at commit on a reintroduced required-but-derivable param or an in-pi-op vs reflecting-CLI output-shape divergence — the op<->CLI parity classes (the orchestrator scripts are not a parity reference; this criterion was reframed from the original op<->script wording when scripts were taken off the table in TASK-019).

Every in-pi op is byte-unchanged except the authorized read-schema correction; the CLI is an additive layer over the canonical op surface (the per-op help examples[] metadata added in TASK-042 is invisible to the in-pi tool surface — registerAll does not project it).

features.title: update blocked-diagnostic + resolution loop — story-driven
features.description: Bring pi-context update's blocked outcome from an opaque dead-end to a diagnosed, resolvable state, and make the dry-run predict the live outcome. The work container for the update-arc gap set: FGAP-066 (dry/live outcome parity), FGAP-077 (per-item validation diagnostic), FGAP-030+FGAP-078 (check-status exposed as a CLI op + version-gap reporting), FGAP-079 (catalog schema locally diffable), FGAP-080 (pending-blocked state + resolve-blocked / update --continue), FGAP-081 (in-file failure markers, object-store recoverable). INVARIANT (every task preserves it): the per-schema byte-exact rollback and per-component application (DEC-0018) are preserved; no data loss on any blocked path; the only blocked-path write is the explicitly-declared marker-mode of FGAP-081, with the pre-marker block recoverable from the object store; in-pi op behavior changes only where a gap names it. The feature's success criteria are the user stories it advances — STORY-001..014, filed verbatim from the operator's in-pi reports — not embedded criteria text.
features.motivation: An operator hit blocked on a live update the dry-run had predicted safe, with no diagnostic naming the failing item/field and no resolution path — the inciting incident the 14 user stories articulate. The standard is best-of-breed: diagnose, fix, retry, with the dry-run telling the truth.
acceptance_criteria: Every user story this feature advances (the feature_advances_story edges -> STORY-001..014) is met — a story is met when a task or feature advancing it has met its success criteria. The stories carry the criteria; this entry references them without duplication.

features.title: Substrate evolution safety + currency — catalog/config/schema evolution and substrate read/repair/materialize never brick or silently corrupt an installed substrate
features.description: Catalog, config, and schema evolution, and every read / repair / materialize of an already-installed substrate, preserve its loadability, the integrity of its install/validity signals, and its recoverability through sanctioned ops. Config carries a versioned envelope and migrates on load across a version gap; an invalid config is recoverable through a sanctioned repair that validates the result, not the pre-existing state; a non-additive config-schema diff is flagged at build time unless paired with a migration; block data carries schema_version so read-time validation and migration are active; a same-version schema resync re-validates existing items and refuses a narrowing change; a block kind declared after install is materialized into the existing substrate. Invariant: no evolution, read, repair, or materialize leaves the substrate bricked, silently corrupted, or unrecoverable through sanctioned ops.
acceptance_criteria: A config whose schema_version lags the bundled config schema is migrated forward on load and loads valid: loadConfigForDir migrates-then-validates via the schema-name-keyed config migration chain.

An invalid config.json is restored to validity through a sanctioned CLI repair path that validates the corrected result rather than refusing on the pre-existing invalid state; recovery needs no forbidden direct edit.
A non-additive config-schema diff (a removed/renamed key or a new required field) is flagged at build time unless paired with a registered migration.
Block data carries schema_version; the read-time validation hook and the version-mismatch migration branch fire for blocks, so a version-bump migration applies to existing block data on read.
A block kind declared in config after a substrate was installed is materialized into that substrate by a sanctioned op; context-bootstrap-state then reports ready and the block is writable.

A same-version catalog schema resync re-validates existing block items against the new body and refuses (blocked, byte-unchanged) when a narrowing change would invalidate them, symmetric with the version-bump path's migrate-then-revalidate-or-refuse.

features.title: Currency by construction — stored substrate state cannot silently diverge from derivable/current truth
features.description: Permanently foreclose non-currency within pi-context using only its own machinery (no external harness), by generalizing the engine's proven schema_version construction — read-normalize + write-converge + fail-loud at the existing write chokes — to substrate state at large, stratified by truth-class: (A) mechanically derivable stored state (milestone status; any state_derivation.rollups kind) converges by construction — gate satisfaction consults the rollup within the same read, engine writes that change a rollup input stamp the affected items' stored status (a converge-stamp is not authoring, the schema_version argument), a derived-status invariant class detects both divergence directions out-of-band, and a context-reconcile op repairs existing substrates with a dry-run-exact preview; (B) authored lifecycle state (feature/gap/issue/task buckets) is never fabricated by the engine — instead the write-time invariant gate, delta-scoped, surfaces a newly-introduced violation at the causal write, and currentState carries always-on drift surfacing; (C) declared-baseline currency (research stale_conditions, citations) becomes machine-evaluable via typed condition variants and content-hash pins captured at the write choke, flagged by validateContext, transitioned via reconcile or human confirmation; (D) free prose gets anchor-drift flags only. Explicit residue, stated not hidden: semantic prose truth, acceptance-criteria judgment, off-repo conditions, and engine-bypassing writes are detectable, never preventable, by any state engine.
features.motivation: The 2026-07-05 currency audit found the classes live: currentState simultaneously derived MILE-003 reached and reported it as an unsatisfied gate (the stored field, forbidden to author, fed gate satisfaction); R-0012 sat complete 25 days after its own filed stale condition fired; FEAT-004/FEAT-010 sat proposed with most criteria shipped; filed gap text overstated after partial fixes. Detection existed only in an on-demand validate run nobody is forced to execute. Design basis: analysis/2026-07-05-currency-foreclosure-shape.md (unprejudiced code-derived seam map, nine candidate shapes with an events/TTL rejection, and this composed recommendation).
acceptance_criteria: Within a single currentState read, gate satisfaction and the milestones[] rollup agree by construction: isCompleted consults the derived rollup for every config-declared rollup kind (state_derivation.rollups), no kind or relation literals in the deriver; a milestone whose phases complete releases its task_gated_by_item gates in the same derivation.

Converge-on-write: an engine write that changes a rollup input (member status, membership edge) recomputes and stamps the affected rollup items' stored status through the standard write path (identity-stamped, validated, attested); the stamp is convergence, not authoring — the stored value equals the derivation after every engine write.

A derived-status invariant class (additive config-schema oneOf branch + engine loop beside the existing two, old engines skip unknown classes) reports stored-vs-derived divergence in BOTH directions for declared rollup kinds; registered dual-surface (live config + samples/conception.json).

A context-reconcile op computes all derived-status deltas, fired typed staleness conditions, and citation-pin drifts; --dryRun predicts the live outcome exactly; the live run applies through the standard write path attested to the invoking writer; reflected in the CLI with parity.

Write-time invariant evaluation at the item-grain chokes, delta-scoped: a write that INTRODUCES a violation absent before it surfaces on the op result (warning severity) or is refused (error severity); pre-existing violations never block writes on legacy substrates; verdicts share the validateContext helpers (identical-verdict, the edge-gate precedent).

Research stale_conditions admit typed variants alongside free strings (item-status / file-changed / revision-moved shapes), and path-bearing citations capture a content-hash pin at write; validateContext flags complete research whose typed condition fired or whose pin drifted as a staleness candidate; the complete-to-stale transition is applied by reconcile or a human, never silently.

currentState surfaces drift always-on: a rollup item whose stored status differs from its derivation carries both values in the result, and a driftWarnings head rides the payload computed from the derivation's own working set — no separate validate run required to see the divergence.

The substrate-derived-state convention is authored in the conventions block (resolving the milestone schema's currently-dangling canon citation) and every new vocabulary lands dual-surface (live config + packaged catalog) — no live-only vocabulary.

The residue is stated on the feature's surfaces: prose truth, criteria judgment, off-repo conditions, and bypass-writes are flagged-for-review classes, not foreclosed classes; no surface claims total foreclosure of semantic currency.

features.title: Cross-substrate consumption — derived state, invariants, and read surfaces resolve foreign endpoints through the registry
features.description: Complete the symmetric peer graph the 2026-05-31 identity spec envisions ('substrates are wholly separate peers… related only by an explicit edge a user files; references are symmetric') by making foreign endpoints CONSUMED, not only filed and certified. The v0.28.0 arc shipped foreign IDENTITY — structured EdgeEndpoint with substrate_id, the project-root registry with aliases, resolveRef active/foreign/dangling/unregistered classification with per-pass foreign-index caching, promote-item lineage — and stopped before consumption: every edge-consuming derivation join (gate/dependency satisfaction, derivedRollupComplete membership completeness, the status-consistency invariant, requires-edge satisfaction, joinBlocks edge mode, roadmap rollups, walk traversals) computes endpointKey (the bare refname, substrate_id discarded) and resolves against the ACTIVE substrate's byRefname index only, so valid foreign endpoints are silently dropped (gates derive false-ready), defaulted (rollups derive false-never-complete, and reconcile/converge stamp that status durably), skipped (invariants), qualifier-erased (walks), or collision-mis-resolved onto a same-refname local item — while validateContext's edge-integrity certifies the same edges as valid. This feature routes every such join through registry-aware endpoint resolution (resolveRef or an equivalent resolveEndpointLoc shim with the existing foreignCache), making cross-substrate edges carry derived consequence: a task gated on an open foreign item derives blocked with the substrate-qualified blocker; a milestone whose foreign member is complete in its home substrate can reach; declared invariants compare foreign targets per spec §J3 (compare-if-readable, warn-if-unreadable). The topologies this serves are the envisioned ones: transitional A/B cutover, per-arc scratch, frozen-substrate consultation, curated carry-forward, and concurrent operational peers with live cross-dependencies — the first use case demanding concurrent-peer derivation.
features.motivation: A downstream two-substrate project hit the gap on first real use: a task gated on an open foreign item derived ready and entered nextActions with validate clean. The upstream verification (analysis/2026-07-06-foreign-status-derivation-blindness.md) confirmed the mechanism at every derivation surface, demonstrated the refname-collision mis-resolution (a local closed same-refname item silently satisfied a foreign open gate — collisions are the EXPECTED case across catalog-installed substrates), and established the split-brain: the write path and integrity surface accept and certify foreign edges that derivation treats as nonexistent. The prior-art sweep found the consumption layer unfiled and undesigned (spec Phase J wired the resolver into validation only; §J3's foreign-aware status-consistency was designed, never implemented).
acceptance_criteria: Gate and dependency satisfaction (currentState incompletePreds/unsatisfiedGates) resolve foreign endpoints through the registry: a task gated on a foreign item that is not complete in its home substrate derives blocked, with the blocker carried substrate-qualified (alias:refname), and releases when the foreign item completes; the two-substrate reproduction fixture from the investigation, promoted to a regression test, passes.

derivedRollupComplete resolves foreign members through the registry — a rollup whose foreign member is complete in its home substrate can derive complete — and its three consumers (currentState gate satisfaction + milestones facet, the derived-status invariant, context-reconcile/converge-on-write) inherit the semantics through the shared helper with no per-consumer divergence.

The status-consistency invariant implements spec §J3 for foreign targets: the status comparison runs when the foreign item's block/status is readable, and an unreadable foreign substrate degrades LOUDLY (a warning naming the unresolvable endpoint), never to a silent skip; requires-edge foreign-satisfaction semantics are examined and recorded in the same decision, not left as fallout.

No derivation or read surface silently drops a foreign endpoint: joinBlocks edge mode, roadmap rollups, and the walk ops either resolve foreign counterparts or surface them explicitly with the substrate qualifier preserved in output; findReferences' structured-endpoint fidelity is pinned as the parity bar.

Refname-collision aliasing is eliminated: a foreign structured endpoint never key-collides onto an active-substrate item with the same refname, on any consuming surface (the normalizeEndpoint key qualifies foreignness or every consumer branches on the foreign flag); the collision reproduction from the investigation becomes a regression test asserting the foreign item's status is read, not the local impostor's.

The dangling-vs-foreign conflation in the gate guard is split: dangling targets keep their documented semantics, foreign targets follow the decided foreign-resolution semantics, and the two are distinguishable in diagnostics.

The foreign-resolution SEMANTICS — live cross-substrate status read vs derive-as-blocked-until-resolved vs flagged-exclusion, including freshness, cost, offline-substrate, and status-vocabulary-trust trade-offs — is recorded as a DECISION with its derivation shown before implementation lands; the implementation matches the decided semantics on every surface (no per-surface divergence).

Any vocabulary or invariant additions land dual-surface (live config + packaged catalog), and the feature's surfaces state the residue honestly: an unregistered or unreadable peer substrate degrades loudly to the decided fallback, never silently.

features.title: The process harness — the canonical pipeline as a mechanical deny surface with a state layer, an ask gate, and user-owned authorization objects
features.description:
Foreclose LLM laziness and chaos-producing relitigation mechanistically: the canonical process stops being policy the model recalls and becomes refusals the harness issues.
The canonical valid process is the per-task state machine SURFACED → INVESTIGATED → FILED → GATE-VALIDATED → EXPLORED → PLANNED → BASELINED → IMPLEMENTED → GATED → DEMOED → PROBED → findings-loop → DOCS-SYNCED → MERGED → CLOSED → HELD-RELEASE (analysis/2026-07-06-harness-requirements.md §1).
Five pillars (mechanism model per analysis/2026-07-06-harness-exemplars-wasc-synth.md):
1. Procedure-as-refusal (R1–R5) — PreToolUse deny hooks close the honor-system chokepoints: direct substrate-JSON writes, --no-verify / destructive git, exit-code masking, off-plan or integration-branch source edits, orchestrator edits during delegated implementation.
2. State layer (R11, R13–R16, R18–R19) — per-work-unit phase state (SessionStart-seeded from substrate truth, event-minted transitions) lets hooks ask "is this call legal in this state"; a findings ledger makes iterate-to-zero mechanical.
3. Stop-hook ask gate (R12) — blocks turn-end on any ask failing the three-legitimate-stops filter; every other ask is relitigation, refused with the rule named.
4. Authorization as object (R2, R4, R6) — the release/push hold becomes a deny-without-user-minted-grant hook; same-turn self-attestation closed by a guard-marker the Stop hook clears.
5. Harness predictability (R3, R7–R9, R17, R20) — wiring moves to tracked .claude/settings.json; mandates.jsonl populated; every guard traces its failure class; the deny surface gets regression cells.
Dispatch prompts for subagents inline the mandates deterministically (WASC renderer pattern) since subagents do not receive the UserPromptSubmit injection.
Reasoned omissions from the WASC exemplar: one-bash-per-turn (no observed failure of that class in this project's record; parallel independent calls are the operating style) and git-tracked rendered prompts (claude-history is this project's forensic layer for dispatch reconstruction).
features.motivation:
User directive (2026-07-06, verbatim): "i cannot rely on claude code deciding to honor and follow requisite policies and conventions on their own, because clearly that is not happening, and chaos producing relitigation is now become more routine. we need to foreclose it mechanistically. you are deviating and the likelihood of further chaos production is a danger to the rational sound valid continuance of work in this project. our first model of harness was the wasc project. we also have a variant example in ../MUSE/SYNTH. I want the predictable non-bypassable harness shaped for this project that encodes the canonical valid process for success such that llm laziness and chaos production ends for good." The observed record: two same-day relitigation corrections (governed timing and iterate-to-zero findings reframed as asks at turn boundaries), and the requirements investigation's finding that everything between user prompt and commit -- investigation discipline, plan mode, subagent-primary implementation, runtime demo, adversarial probe, iterate-to-zero, docs audit, ask-vs-proceed, release hold -- is honor-system, with the hook wiring itself untracked.

Follow-on user directive (2026-07-10, verbatim): "what I don't want is for this to be the llm punt escape hatch: 'If a subagent returns an issue, STOP. Report to user. User decides next action.' That needs to be governed by policy, not the laziness-inviting 'user decides next action.'" R12 must therefore explicitly treat a subagent-issue stop (mandate-008) as legitimate only when it also passes the three-legitimate-stops filter, not as a bare invocation right. Basis for the shape: WASC's canonical-pipeline skill (../wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan/.claude/skills/canonical-pipeline/SKILL.md) scopes its own mandate-008 to the IMPL step, paired with a plan-mode gate that keeps unresolved ambiguity from ever reaching IMPL and audit-side enforcement that flags an illegitimate stop as a finding; analysis/2026-06-21-wasc-out-of-scope-stop-firing-rate.md found 0% of 70 completed WASC loop runs stopped mid-stream -- every fired stop was a pre-audit hard boundary, never ambiguity-avoidance.
acceptance_criteria: Deny surface live (R1–R5). Four PreToolUse guards block, each with regression cells (specifics per TASK-094/095/097): (a) substrate-JSON direct writes; (b) --no-verify + destructive git without sentinel; (c) pipe/truncate-masked gate commands; (d) off-plan or integration-branch packages/** edits.

The same-turn sentinel hole is closed (R6): a guard-fired marker set on any provenance-guard block is cleared only by the Stop hook at a genuine turn boundary, and a sentinel-bearing re-issue before the marker clears is blocked.

Filing completeness is checked at the guard (R7-R8): a framework-gaps append without an investigation-report citation in its evidence is blocked; deferral/scope-reduction lexical markers in filings and plan text draw a non-blocking nudge naming the governing mandate.

The per-prompt governance layer is populated (R9): .claude/mandates.jsonl carries the project-shaped mandates (pipeline-first, explore-agents-only, three-stops ask gate, iterate-to-zero) through the existing UserPromptSubmit injection slot, and subagent dispatch prompts inline them deterministically.

The phase-state layer exists (R11): a machine-readable per-work-unit pipeline state seeded/reconciled at SessionStart from substrate truth (never from narrative), transitions minted by hook-verified events (ExitPlanMode → PLANNED, feature-branch commit → IMPLEMENTED, complete-task → CLOSED), consulted by the deny hooks.

The Stop-hook ask gate is live (R12): a turn ending on an ask that fails the three-legitimate-stops filter (a pending provenance grant, an escalation naming why the choice is underdetermined, a HELD action awaiting authorization) is blocked with the governing convention/mandate named; the three legitimate stops always pass.

Iterate-to-zero is mechanical (R13-R14): probe/demo findings land in a findings ledger; git merge into the integration branch and complete-task are blocked while the ledger holds open entries; closure requires recorded demo evidence and a fresh-context probe verdict; the user's recorded deprioritization is the only other closing path.

Implementation discipline is enforced (R15-R16, R10): orchestrator source edits while the phase state says implementation is delegated are blocked (user-sentinel escape per mandate-005's explicit-direction carve-out); orchestrator investigation-reads outside the claim-verification window draw a nudge; a PostToolUse(Agent) hook injects the subagent-claim verification checklist.

HELD actions are mechanically held (R4): npm run release:*, npm publish, and non-durably-authorized git push are blocked without a user-minted grant object; the grant is user-attributed and expires with its scope.

The harness itself is predictable (R17-R20): hook registrations live in tracked .claude/settings.json; a session driver walks a work unit through the state machine as the path of least resistance; the milestone-validity-gate check fires at plan approval for milestone-member tasks once its record type exists; every guard's refusal text names the rule and the failure class it forecloses; the deny surface carries its own regression cells (true-positives still block, legitimate work passes).
The three legitimate stops are preserved everywhere: no mechanism auto-grants a provenance ceremony, auto-decides an honestly-underdetermined scope/value judgment, or auto-proceeds a HELD action.

The detection half is live (the WASC exemplar's dropped complement, TASK-102): a read-only currency auditor runs on an interval (/loop convention) with an advance-only-on-clean watermark — a non-clean verdict never advances the baseline, so every finding recurs each cycle until fixed and notifies the user exactly once per run; a clean run is silent and advances the baseline; the loop detects missing REQUIRED actions (unclosed shipped work, unflipped buckets, fired stale-conditions, skipped prerequisite filings) that refusal hooks structurally cannot see. Basis: analysis/2026-07-07-wasc-harness-delta-why-requisite-steps-still-optional.md.

features.title: Work-order dispatch executes real work-orders — the v1 schema contract gets its engine
features.description:
The work-orders schema (catalog-installable, v1) declares a complete dispatch contract: typed input, context-block injection, output validation, operation-scoped capability clamp, deterministic real-checks, bounded retry. The shipped engine implements a demo slice on a dispatch primitive that cannot act: executeAgent is a single-turn completion primitive that never binds tool implementations, the loop consumes only 5 of the 9 required fields, no ceremony seeds the agents tier the loader resolves from, the sanctioned agent-authoring tool is unreachable non-interactively, and non-interactive failure is mislabeled as a human abort. This feature closes the contract-engine gap so a filed work-order runs end to end from the loop's own operating context (pi -p non-interactive included).
First decomposition step is a DECISION on the dispatch architecture (the underdetermined choice from analysis/2026-07-07-work-order-dispatch-dogfood-breaks.md §cross-cutting): (a) dispatch target agents as pi subprocesses (where real tools exist; executeAgent stays a classify/structured-output primitive), (b) grow executeAgent a real tool-execution loop, or (c) re-scope work-orders to output-only agents (contradicts the schema's scope contract). Implementation follows the decided shape.
features.motivation: User-directed dogfooding probe (2026-07-07): WO-001, a minimal real work-order, was driven through run-work-order-loop from pi -p. The dispatched agent received ZERO executable tools (empirically instrumented: grant ['write','bash'] passed the DEC-0047 clamp and was discarded; the child LLM got a bare prompt), so its 'cannot write files' statement was accurate; the deterministic real-check correctly failed; the retry gate silently auto-declined and recorded 'aborted-by-human'. Every honest work-order must fail this way today. Investigation of record: analysis/2026-07-07-work-order-dispatch-dogfood-breaks.md.
acceptance_criteria: The dispatch-architecture decision (subprocess vs executor tool-loop vs output-only re-scope) is filed as a decision before implementation; the implementation matches it on every surface.
A dispatched target agent can act: the WO-001 probe recipe (a work-order whose agent must write a file, verified by a runtime-demo real-check) passes end to end, non-interactively.

The engine honors every schema-required field: input validated against input_contract, the work-order's context_blocks injected, output validated against output_contract, the capability clamp intersecting wo.scope.operations — or the schema is amended to stop asserting engine behavior that was decided out, dual-surface.

A fresh substrate can produce a resolvable target_agent from the loop's operating context: the agents tier is seeded by ceremony or a bundled tier is wired, and agent authoring has a sanctioned non-interactive path or a filed caller-as-reconciler policy (FGAP-068 shape).

Non-interactive runs report honest statuses (no environment default recorded as a human decision) and carry an explicit opt-in retry policy; the gate-coverage inconsistency is closed (a composite op never embeds an effect its gated ceremony tool protects without the gate).
Regression: the probe recipe is a pinned test; the deterministic real-check verdict path stays exit-code/artifact-parsed, never LLM self-report.
